generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance DataInterpretationAgent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Software agent that processes and interprets incoming data from physical system to derive insights or trigger actions. Implements rule engines or AI/ML modules for contextual analysis."
]

instance BehavioralSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Simulation component leveraging MathWorks Simulink or other simulation tools to model and validate system behavior under different scenarios."
]

instance AutonomousDecisionModule : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Module enabling closed-loop operations by pushing data back to physical system for control, optimization, or predictive maintenance."
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization component providing unified views combining SysML, ADLs, and simulation outputs for heterogeneous stakeholders."
]

instance ContextualAbstractionEngine : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregates and abstracts data traces from sensors, logs, or other sources into actionable insights for predictive analytics, anomaly detection, or performance optimization."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components (implied from Digital Shadow context)
instance ManufacturingProcessSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational states, environmental parameters, or performance metrics from manufacturing processes as part of Digital Shadow implementation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ManufacturingProcessDataTransmitted
]

instance SystemStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors physical system state including sensor readings, actuator positions, and operational parameters for Digital Twin integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SystemStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ManufacturingProcessDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingProcessSensor
    base:desc "Automated one-way data flow from manufacturing processes including sensor readings, operational states, and performance metrics via industry-standard protocols."
]

instance SystemStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SystemStateSensor
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical system including sensor readings, environmental parameters, and system performance metrics."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RequirementsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured requirements layer modeled using MBSE methodologies like RFLP or OOSEM across multiple abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional architecture description using SysML or ADLs defining system functions and their interconnections."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcADL
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Logical architecture model representing system components, interfaces, and composition using SysML IBDs or MontiArc."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AADLTool
]

instance ProductTechnicalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Product/technical layer model incorporating physics-based simulations and detailed component specifications."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

instance BehavioralStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State-based behavioral model using SysML state machines for dynamic system behavior representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance PhysicalSystemData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and preprocessed data from physical system traces including filtered, normalized, and validated sensor/actuator data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingProcessDataTransmitted, SystemStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationLayer
]

instance SimulationResultsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output data from simulation engines representing system behavior under various scenarios and operational conditions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SystemStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationAnalysisTool
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based toolchain for requirements, functional/logical architecture, and behavioral modeling including state machines."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemDesignService, StakeholderVisualizationService
]

instance MontiArcADL : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Architecture Description Language for component-based system design with interface, connector, and composition modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentCompositionService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance AADLTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL tool for architecture-centric modeling of embedded systems with focus on component interconnections and deployment."
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemIntegrationService, SafetyAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink for dynamic system simulation, control system design, and signal processing integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance DataAggregationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component filtering, transforming, and abstracting raw sensor/actuator data into actionable models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService, ContextualAnalysisService
]

instance SimulationAnalysisTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, ValidationService
]

instance CppIntegrationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables CustomAlgorithmService, SystemControlService
]

instance JavaIntegrationLayer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance MessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is implicitly addressed through the **orchestration and coordination** of system models using **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT). The document emphasizes **multi-perspective integration** (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers) via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc, which define **component integration**, **interfaces**, and **interconnections**. For **behavioral coordination**, tools like **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** (v1.7+) are employed, while **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) or **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) handle detailed dynamics. **Resource management** is not explicitly detailed, but the **heterogeneous toolchain** (e.g., SysML + Simulink) suggests **modular orchestration** with potential **service-based decomposition**. The document does not specify **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** beyond ADL-based composition."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity requirements** aligned with MBSE methodologies, ensuring models reflect the **physical system’s state and behavior** with sufficient accuracy for their intended purpose. **Validation and verification (V&V) methods** are implied through the use of **simulation tools (e.g., Simulink)** and **formal modeling languages (e.g., SysML state machines, MontiArc Automaton)**, which enable **behavioral validation** against expected system dynamics. Uncertainty sources are mitigated via **data aggregation and abstraction** from digital shadows, though explicit quantification mechanisms (e.g., statistical error propagation, probabilistic modeling) are not detailed. **Error detection and correction** rely on **active DT components** processing incoming data traces, while **quality assurance** is ensured through **multi-layered modeling consistency checks** (e.g., cross-referencing requirements, functional, and logical architectures). The system’s fidelity is further constrained by the **passive nature of digital shadows**, which lack autonomous feedback loops, necessitating external software components for interpretation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. However, the context implies that the DT must integrate with **heterogeneous modeling tools and stakeholders**, suggesting a need for **scalable, flexible deployment models** (e.g., cloud, edge, or hybrid architectures). While specific **hosting platforms** (e.g., AWS IoT, Microsoft Azure Digital Twins, or on-premise solutions) are not mentioned, the system must support **computational and storage requirements** aligned with the complexity of the digital model, including **simulation tools** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink [28], other simulation environments [29]–[30]) and **general-purpose programming languages** (e.g., C++, Java). **Scalability mechanisms** are implied to handle varying workloads, and **availability/reliability assurance** would rely on redundancy and fault-tolerant designs, though no specifics are provided. The document emphasizes the need to **integrate diverse perspectives and tools**, hinting at a **modular deployment approach** to accommodate MBSE methodologies like RFLP, OOSEM, or SMArDT [18]–[22]."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on **horizontal integration** with external systems. However, it implies **interoperability** through the use of **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **heterogeneous toolchains** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java), which require **data exchange** between modeling notations. While no **specific protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) or **standards** (e.g., FIWARE, Industry 4.0) are mentioned, the **component & connector perspective** of ADLs (e.g., ACME, AADL) suggests **interface-based integration** with external systems. The document also references **digital shadows** as a precursor to DTs, implying **one-way data flows** (e.g., from physical objects to digital models), but does not elaborate on **bidirectional integration** or **dependencies** on enterprise systems or other DTs."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** standards, leveraging **SysML** (Systems Modeling Language) as a core specification for system modeling across abstraction layers. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** provide standardized frameworks for component-based system descriptions, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling may align with **SysML v1.7 state machines** or **MontiArc Automaton** for consistency in dynamic system representation. While no explicit **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** are mentioned, the use of **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) implies adherence to structured engineering practices. **Interoperability** is addressed through integration with tools like **MathWorks Simulink** and general-purpose languages (**C++**, **Java**), though specific interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) are not referenced in the document."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the **physical safety cell**, which restricts the operational range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** to prevent collisions or unsafe movements. **Threat mitigation** is implicitly addressed via **redundant sensor validation**: the system employs **two position encoders per drive**—one mounted directly on the motor and another behind the gearbox—to detect **backlash and positional uncertainty** by analyzing the differential signal between them. This allows real-time compensation for **elasticity and friction** in the drivetrain, ensuring positional accuracy of **±0.1 mm** and repeatability of **±0.06 mm**. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are partially implied by the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, which enforces **hardware-level constraints** (e.g., maximum speed of **75°/s**) and provides **closed-loop control** for position, speed, and acceleration. However, explicit **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., authentication, access control, or encryption) are **not documented**. The system lacks details on **data governance**, **privacy protection**, or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001), as well as **consent management** or **data anonymization** for shared datasets."
]
